## What is Pipedrive?

Pipedrive is a CRM platform designed around the concept of activity-based selling — the idea that consistent, logged actions drive deals forward. Its visual drag-and-drop pipeline is the central UI, and everything from email to automation is organized around moving deals through defined stages.

## Who is it for?

Small to mid-sized sales teams, particularly those new to CRM or migrating from spreadsheets. It scales reasonably well up to a few hundred users, though very large or complex organizations often outgrow it and move to Salesforce or HubSpot.

## Strengths

- **Visual pipeline UI** — intuitive Kanban-style deal view that most reps can use on day one.
- **Activity tracking** — calls, emails, tasks, and meetings are logged against contacts and deals automatically.
- **Email integration** — two-way sync with Gmail and Outlook; AI-assisted email writing on Professional tier and above.
- **Automation** — workflow automation available from the Advanced plan; covers lead assignment, notifications, and field updates.
- **Large integration marketplace** — 300+ native integrations plus Zapier/Make for broader connectivity.

## What to consider

- Reporting and forecasting are limited on lower tiers; meaningful analytics require the Professional plan or higher.
- Add-on costs (LeadBooster, Campaigns, Web Visitors) can raise the effective per-seat price significantly.
- Not designed for complex B2B account hierarchies or post-sales customer success workflows.
- Monthly billing is roughly 21% more expensive than annual; costs add up quickly for growing teams.

## Bottom line

Pipedrive is one of the most accessible sales CRMs available and a strong default choice for teams that want a clean, pipeline-centric tool without a steep learning curve. Evaluate the Professional plan to unlock the automation and reporting that make it genuinely powerful.
